source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Dropped Object Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Desoto Canyon Block 4

actions_taken: Incident reported to BSEE at 20:45 hours
Lessons learned report prepared by Transocean
event.summary: On June 25, 2019, a 162 lb packer setting tool detached from the wireline rope socket and free fell approximately 40 ft to the rig floor during well completion operations on the Deepwater Asgard Drillship. The incident was caused by a breakdown in communication between the winch operator and E-line operator during rigging down operations. The winch operator slacked off before the E-line operator, causing the lubricator weight to set down on the packer setting tool, which allowed the electric line to pull free from the rope socket. No injuries occurred; damage was limited to an indentation in the drill floor and a wooden plank.
recommendations: Use a tool trap on the lubricator to trap the wireline tool string in the event of accidental wire stripping from the rope socket/cable head
Improve radio communication to be more descriptive, identifying who is speaking and to whom the command is directed

hazards.name: Suspended load (lubricator and wireline tool string)
Incompatible rig configuration change (slickline to E-line)

threats.name: Breakdown in communication between winch operator and E-line operator
Winch operator slacking off before E-line operator
Lubricator weight setting down on packer setting tool
Electric line pulling free from rope socket

consequences.name: Dropped object (162 lb packer setting tool)

controls.name: Toolbox talk / pre-job safety briefing
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify and communicate hazards before work begins

supporting_text: the crew did not discuss this hazard during the toolbox talk
The new crew coming on tour did not recognize this hazard

controls.name: Radio communication protocol
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure clear communication during coordinated lifting operations

supporting_text: The floor hands, drill shack personnel, the E-line operator, and the Winch operator were in radio communication
During the rigging down operations and before the incident, a breakdown in communication occurred

controls.name: Operator coordination and sequencing procedure
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper sequence of winch and E-line operator actions
controls.lod_basis: Procedure to control the sequence of load lowering to prevent lubricator weight from setting on tool string

supporting_text: If the Winch operator slacks off before the E-line operator, the lubricator weight will be set down on top of the tool string
The Winch operator started slacking off before the E-line operator

controls.name: Rope socket design and integrity
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to prevent accidental detachment of wireline from rope socket

supporting_text: the electric line to pull free from the rope socket
The broken rope socket allowed the 162 lb packer setting tool (the spear) to free fall 40 ft to the rig floor

controls.name: Tool trap on lubricator
controls.barrier_role: Prevent dropped object by trapping wireline tool string
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to catch and retain wireline tool in event of rope socket failure

supporting_text: Use a tool trap on the lubricator. This tool will trap the wireline tool string within the lubricator in the event of the wire being accidentally stripped from the rope socket/cable head

controls.name: Hazard recognition and crew awareness training
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards introduced by rig configuration change
controls.lod_basis: Training and awareness to recognize hazards in modified operational procedures

supporting_text: The new crew coming on tour did not recognize this hazard
Due to the change in the wireline rig up, the process to lay down the wireline tools and equipment (i.e. lubricator) becomes more complicated

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
